title: Typing Deep Dive
subtitle: Generics, TypeVar, and mypy — static checks, opt-in

sections.java: // Generic method with a bounded type public <T> T first(List<T> xs) { return xs.get(0); } List<String> names = ...; String s = first(names);
sections.python: # TypeVar = the <T> type parameter from typing import TypeVar T = TypeVar("T") def first(xs: list[T]) -> T: return xs[0] s = first(["a", "b"]) # inferred as str

sections.title: Hints Are Erased Too — Checked by mypy, Not the Interpreter
sections.content: Like Java generics (erased at runtime), Python type hints are ignored by the interpreter — they don't enforce anything when your code runs. A separate static checker (mypy or pyright) reads them and flags mismatches before you ship. Run mypy --strict src/ in CI to get compile-time-style safety. reveal_type(x) makes mypy print the inferred type during a check.

sections.java: // Optional, function types, wildcards Optional<User> find(Long id); Function<Integer, String> fmt; Map<String, Object> raw; List<? extends Number> nums;
sections.python: # Modern syntax: | unions, builtin generics from typing import Callable, Any def find(id: int) -> User | None: ... # Optional fmt: Callable[[int], str] # Function raw: dict[str, Any] # Map<String,Object> nums: list[float]

sections.title: Gradual Typing: Add Hints Where They Pay Off
sections.content: You don't have to type everything. Python's gradual typing lets you annotate public APIs and tricky logic while leaving glue code untyped — Any opts out locally. Start with hints on function signatures (the highest-value spot), turn on mypy, and tighten over time. Pair with Protocol (Module 06) for structural interfaces and TypedDict for dict-shaped JSON payloads.

sections.question: When are Python type hints actually enforced?
options: At runtime, on every function call
Never by the interpreter — a static checker like mypy verifies them ahead of time
sections.explanation: The CPython interpreter ignores annotations at runtime (just as the JVM erases generics). Their value comes from static analysis — mypy/pyright in your editor and CI catch type mismatches before execution. They're checked documentation, not runtime guards (libraries like pydantic add runtime validation separately).
